references.title: Predicting academic performance of students with machine learning

extracted.Data sources: Learner Demographics, Submission Dates and Scores on Submissions, LMS logs
extracted.Features: [Student Evaluations, Student Identifiers, Submission Date, Scores]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Data was standardized and categories were one-hot encoded.
extracted.Models: Decision Tree, MLP, SVM, Random Forest
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {F1-Score: 0.8126 - RF, Precision: 0.82 - RF, Recall: 0.81 - RF, Accuracy: 0.83 - RF}
extracted.Comments:
Paper is very poorly written but essencially it is predictive modelling for 3 distinct targets.

The models described as Ensemble Methods outperform the remaining models.

Authors use ANOVA to check whether the obtained performance scores are significantly different or not.
extracted.Limitations:
Undisclosed Feature set

No cross-validation. 

No work on early prediction being done. 

Unclear form of model evaluation → CV and Holdout results are shown.

Single Dataset

annotations.text: The work attempts to use OULAD to predict student performance using 3 distinct categories. The Ensemble Model performed better than all other models attempted. The study not only makes predictions but also delves into discussing pedagogical policies and guidelines that can be derived from these early predictions.
